children.heading: Categorical exclusion established
children.label: b Categorical exclusion established
children.text: Forest management activities described in subsection (c) are a category of actions designated as being categorically excluded from the preparation of an environmental assessment or an environmental impact statement under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) if the categorical exclusion is documented through a supporting record and decision memorandum.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Forest management activities designated for categorical exclusion
children.label: c Forest management activities designated for categorical exclusion
A
children.text: up to 1,000 feet in width contiguous with or incorporating existing linear features, such as roads, water infrastructure, transmission and distribution lines, and pipelines of any length on Federal land; and

children.label: B
children.text: intended to reduce the risk of uncharacteristic wildfire on Federal land or catastrophic wildfire for an adjacent at-risk community.

children.label: A
children.text: mowing or masticating;

children.label: B
children.text: thinning by manual and mechanical cutting;

children.label: C
children.text: piling, yarding, and removal of slash or hazardous fuels;

children.label: D
children.text: selling of vegetation products, including timber, firewood, biomass, slash, and fenceposts;

children.label: E
children.text: targeted grazing;

children.label: i
children.text: pesticide;

children.label: ii
children.text: biopesticide; or

children.label: iii
children.text: herbicide;

children.label: G
children.text: seeding of native species;

children.label: H
children.text: controlled burns and broadcast burning; and

children.label: I
children.text: burning of piles, including jackpot piles.

children.label: A
children.text: in a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System;

children.label: B
children.text: on Federal land on which the removal of vegetation is prohibited or restricted by Act of Congress, Presidential proclamation (including the applicable implementation plan), or regulation;

children.label: C
children.text: in a wilderness study area; or

children.label: D
children.text: in an area in which carrying out the activity would be inconsistent with the applicable land management plan or resource management plan.

children.heading: Extraordinary circumstances
children.label: 4 Extraordinary circumstances
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall apply the extraordinary circumstances procedures under section 220.6 of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation), in determining whether to use a categorical exclusion under subsection (b).

children.text: the wildland-urban interface or a public drinking water source area;

children.label: B
children.text: if located outside the wildland-urban interface or a public drinking water source area, an area within Condition Class 2 or 3 in Fire Regime Group I, II, or III that contains very high wildfire hazard potential; or

children.heading: Permanent roads
children.label: 1 Permanent roads
children.text: A project under this section shall not include the establishment of permanent roads.

children.heading: Existing roads
children.label: 2 Existing roads
children.text: The Secretary concerned may carry out necessary maintenance and repairs on existing permanent roads for the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Temporary roads
children.label: 3 Temporary roads
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall decommission any temporary road constructed under a project under this section not later than 3 years after the date on which the project is completed.
collaboration among State and local governments and Indian Tribes; and
participation of interested persons.
